title: Cochlearia macrorrhiza
taxonomy: Cochlearia macrorrhiza is classified within the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf structure and flower arrangement, which are similar to those of other members of the family. Cochlearia macrorrhiza is further classified as a species within the genus Cochlearia, which comprises a group of plants commonly known as snapperr or scurvy grass. The genus Cochlearia is part of the order Brassicales, which includes a wide range of flowering plants. This classification helps to group plants with similar characteristics and evolutionary histories together.

intro: Cochlearia macrorrhiza, also known as the large-leaved cochlearia or large-leaved scurvy grass, is a perennial plant species in the family Brassicaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including treating scurvy, digestive issues, and skin problems, due to its high content of vitamin C and other antioxidant compounds. The bioactive constituents of Cochlearia macrorrhiza include glycosides, flavonoids, and isothiocyanates, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cochlearia macrorrhiza is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
